answer:
Let $A$, $B$, and $V$ be the sets of problems solved by Anton, Artem, and Vera, respectively. We are given that $|A| = |B| = |V| = 60$. The total number of problems solved is $|A \cup B \cup V| = 100$.
We know that $|A \cup B \cup V| = |A| + |B| + |V| - |A \cap B| - |A \cap V| - |B \cap V| + |A \cap B \cap V|$.
So, $100 = 60 + 60 + 60 - |A \cap B| - |A \cap V| - |B \cap V| + |A \cap B \cap V|$.
$100 = 180 - |A \cap B| - |A \cap V| - |B \cap V| + |A \cap B \cap V|$.
$|A \cap B| + |A \cap V| + |B \cap V| - |A \cap B \cap V| = 80$.

Let $D$ be the set of difficult problems, i.e., problems solved by exactly one person.
Let $E$ be the set of easy problems, i.e., problems solved by all three people.
We have $D = (A \setminus (B \cup V)) \cup (B \setminus (A \cup V)) \cup (V \setminus (A \cup B))$.
Also, $E = A \cap B \cap V$.
The number of difficult problems is $|D|$.
The number of easy problems is $|E|$.
We want to find $|D| - |E|$.

Let $x = |A \cap B|$, $y = |A \cap V|$, $z = |B \cap V|$.
Let $w = |A \cap B \cap V|$.
Then $x + y + z - w = 80$.
The number of problems solved by exactly one person is
$|D| = |A| + |B| + |V| - 2(|A \cap B| + |A \cap V| + |B \cap V|) + 3|A \cap B \cap V|$
$|D| = 60 + 60 + 60 - 2(x + y + z) + 3w$
$|D| = 180 - 2(x + y + z) + 3w$.
The number of easy problems is $|E| = w$.
So, $|D| - |E| = 180 - 2(x + y + z) + 3w - w = 180 - 2(x + y + z) + 2w$.
Since $x + y + z - w = 80$, we have $x + y + z = 80 + w$.
$|D| - |E| = 180 - 2(80 + w) + 2w = 180 - 160 - 2w + 2w = 20$.

Final Answer: The final answer is $\boxed{20}$
